text: What if the future of a species depended on just a few individuals? This is the founder effect, a concept in genetics that describes how a small group can shape a population's traits. Imagine a few settlers starting a new colony. Their genetic traits become dominant over generations. In isolated populations like islands, the founder effect can lead to high frequencies of genetic disorders. So, while a small sample can create diversity, it can also limit it drastically. Curious about evolution? Tap subscribe for more fascinating science!
